desc: When an r-for loop variable is named `e` (or any other identifier that previously collided with the synthetic event-handler closure param), the user expression in @click="..." must resolve `e.id` to the OUTER loop scope, NOT the synthetic event. Pre-fix: React/Svelte/Solid/Lit emitted `(e) => { removeItem(e.id); }` where `e` was the SyntheticEvent → runtime undefined. Post-fix: closure param renamed to `$event`, so user-written `e.id` resolves to the loop var. Vue/Angular were already correct because their template bindings don't introduce a JS closure scope.
